old_value: {"id":"harmony-openwrt-im5","title":"Auto-join HARMONY-MESH communal WiFi network","description":"Every Harmony-OpenWRT device should automatically create/join an 802.11s mesh interface with SSID 'HARMONY-MESH' and PSK 'ZEBLITHIC'. This is intentionally well-known — Harmony provides E2E encryption (Reticulum + ML-KEM), so the WiFi layer is just transport. The mesh should auto-configure on install: add the mesh VIF to the 5GHz radio, set mesh_fwding=0 (Reticulum handles routing), set mcast_rate=24000. This replaces the current manual setup guide with zero-touch mesh peering. Any Harmony device within radio range automatically joins the mesh.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T03:07:24Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T03:07:24Z"}

{"id":"harmony-openwrt-4jl","title":"Firewall-free WAN for Harmony protocols","description":"Remove the WAN firewall restriction for Harmony traffic. The current firewall blocks UDP 4242 on WAN — but with Harmony's E2E encryption (Reticulum, ML-KEM, ML-DSA), there is no meaningful trust difference between LAN and WAN. Any Harmony/Zenoh/Reticulum peer on the other side of the WAN port should 'just work'. Implementation: allow Harmony protocol traffic (UDP 4242 for Reticulum, Zenoh scouting ports, iroh-net QUIC) through the WAN zone. The firewall disappears for Harmony connections while remaining active for non-Harmony traffic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T03:07:25Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T03:07:25Z"}

new_value: Auto-join HARMONY-MESH implemented: uci-defaults auto-detects 5GHz radio, creates mesh VIF with well-known credentials and optimized tunings.
Firewall-free WAN implemented: UDP 4242 (Reticulum), 7446 (Zenoh), 4433-4435 (iroh-net) open on both LAN and WAN zones.

old_value: {"id":"harmony-openwrt-ua3","title":"Broadcast-optimized WiFi for CAS/NDN content sharing","description":"On the HARMONY-MESH network, broadcast traffic is useful — CAS/NDN means overhearing content announcements, Bloom filters, and Zenoh scouting benefits all participants. Configure the mesh interface for broadcast-friendly operation: elevated mcast_rate, optimized beacon interval, tuned multicast-to-unicast settings. Investigate whether Zenoh's multicast scouting can leverage WiFi broadcast directly (UDP multicast → WiFi broadcast) without IP layer overhead. Research: can we use raw 802.11 data frames for Zenoh/Reticulum instead of IP, making the broadcast domain a native content-addressed network?","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T03:07:24Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T03:07:24Z"}

{"id":"harmony-openwrt-1mv","title":"Update PKG_SOURCE_VERSION to latest harmony main","description":"PKG_SOURCE_VERSION in harmony-node/Makefile points to 2fc9f06f, which predates tunnel infrastructure, discovery, JCS, credential import, and dozens of other features. Update to latest main commit and rebuild.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T03:06:28Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T03:06:28Z"}

{"id":"harmony-openwrt-yw6","title":"Disable power save on mesh interface","description":"802.11 power save causes DTIM buffering delays for broadcast/multicast frames. Harmony mesh nodes are always-on routers — power save is actively harmful. Add 'iw dev mesh0 set power_save off' to the init script or uci-defaults. Simple but important for broadcast latency.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T03:42:03Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T03:42:03Z"}

{"id":"harmony-openwrt-979","title":"Isolate HARMONY-MESH from br-lan with dedicated network/firewall zone","description":"The mesh VIF currently bridges to br-lan (network='lan'), giving any device with the well-known PSK full LAN access. Create a dedicated 'harmony' network interface with its own subnet, DHCP pool, and firewall zone that only permits Harmony protocol ports (UDP 4242, 7446, 4434-4435) and blocks access to other LAN services. Requires harmony-node to bind to the mesh interface specifically or route between zones.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:15Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:15Z"}

new_value: All dependencies closed: auto-mesh (im5), firewall (4jl), broadcast (ua3). Plus power save (yw6) and network isolation (979) shipped.

old_value: {"id":"harmony-openwrt-atf","title":"Tunnel and relay UCI configuration","description":"The UCI config has no options for --relay-url, --tunnel-peer, or tunnel entries. These are needed for internet-connected mesh nodes that peer over iroh-net tunnels. Add relay_url, tunnel_peer list options to /etc/config/harmony-node and wire them into the init script.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T03:06:58Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T03:06:58Z"}

{"id":"harmony-openwrt-bte","title":"x86_64 target support","description":"DEPENDS:=@(aarch64) restricts to aarch64 only. Many OpenWRT devices and VMs are x86_64. Add x86_64-unknown-linux-musl as a supported target, make architecture selectable in the Makefile.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T03:06:57Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T03:06:57Z"}

{"id":"harmony-openwrt-9bl","title":"RSSI threshold for mesh peering quality","description":"Add mesh_rssi_threshold to reject peering with weak-signal nodes. Complements mcast_rate=24000 which culls weak links at the frame level — RSSI threshold prevents peering establishment in the first place. Needs research on appropriate dBm value for 5GHz outdoor/indoor mesh.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T06:16:08Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T06:16:08Z"}

{"id":"harmony-openwrt-a3c","title":"BSS Coloring for dense mesh deployments","description":"Configure a unified BSS color across all Harmony mesh nodes to enable spatial reuse (802.11ax). Nodes treat alien transmissions from neighboring non-Harmony networks as background noise, reducing unnecessary CSMA/CA backoff. Requires Wi-Fi 6 hardware (MT7996). Add he_bss_color UCI option to auto-config.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T03:42:04Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T03:42:04Z"}

{"id":"harmony-openwrt-6e1","title":"Generate TOML config from UCI","description":"harmony-node now supports TOML config files (harmony-r4o). The init script should generate a TOML config from UCI options instead of passing CLI flags, enabling richer configuration (nested structures, lists) that CLI flags can't express.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"zeblith@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-24T03:06:58Z","created_by":"Jake Englund","updated_at":"2026-03-24T03:06:58Z"}
